AvailabilitySlot.description:
One row returned by the public_get_product_availability RPC.
Mirrors the widget's ``AvailabilitySlot`` TypeScript interface
(src/api/types.ts) field-for-field.
AvailabilitySlot.title: AvailabilitySlot

BdIntentIn.description:
Request body for the file-a-bd-ticket endpoint.

title:    Title for the bd issue (required, non-empty after strip).
bd_type:  bd issue type — one of: bug | feature | task.
priority: bd priority string — one of: 0 | 1 | 2 (0 = highest).
body:     Engineering body / prompt for the bd issue (optional but
          strongly recommended — the drain worker passes this as the
          bd create body).
BdIntentIn.title: BdIntentIn

BdIntentOut.description: Response after successfully queuing a bd_intent.
BdIntentOut.title: BdIntentOut

BookingCalendarEvent.description:
Parsed calendar event for client-side Google Calendar / Outlook deep-links.

landr-odsr: mirrors the widget's ``BookingCalendarEvent`` TypeScript interface
(src/api/types.ts) exactly — field names must not diverge or the confirmation
screen goes blank (landr-9ut4).
BookingCalendarEvent.title: BookingCalendarEvent

BookingNoteIn.description:
POST body — the note text. Whitespace-only rejected at the model
level (the DB CHECK constraint catches it too, but a 422 from Pydantic
is the cleaner client surface).
BookingNoteIn.title: BookingNoteIn

BriefingDayIn.description: PUT body for one day card — the operator's nightly update.
BriefingDayIn.title: BriefingDayIn

BriefingPatchIn.description: PATCH body. All fields optional; only those set are written.
BriefingPatchIn.title: BriefingPatchIn

CampaignIn.description:
Create payload for a new campaign.

`code` and `label` are required; `kind` is required (no sensible
default). `scope` defaults to 'booking' to match the DB default.
`end_date` is optional (open-ended campaigns); when present it must
not precede `start_date`.
CampaignIn.title: CampaignIn

CampaignPatch.description:
Partial update — every field optional.

`code` is patchable (operators rename mis-typed codes); a collision
with another active row surfaces as 409. The start/end window check
only runs when both ends are supplied in the same patch — a partial
patch that touches only one end can't be validated here without a
read-modify-write, so the DB stays the backstop (no CHECK exists, so
we accept a one-sided patch and trust the caller / future hardening).
CampaignPatch.title: CampaignPatch

CheckinIn.description: POST body for a customer check-in.
CheckinIn.title: CheckinIn

ChecklistTemplateItem.description:
One row in the operator's default checklist.

`key` is a stable identifier so renaming `label` in code (or in the
operator's UI) doesn't orphan per-booking done flags keyed on the
same id in localStorage v1. `order` is a non-negative int — the
dashboard sorts ascending. Duplicate keys within a single PUT
are rejected at the model level (see ChecklistTemplateUpdate).
ChecklistTemplateItem.title: ChecklistTemplateItem

ChecklistTemplateUpdate.description:
PUT body — replaces the operator's template wholesale.

Empty list IS valid: an operator who wants zero default items
(e.g. they fully drive checklists via per-booking custom items
in v1) should be able to clear the seed list.
ChecklistTemplateUpdate.title: ChecklistTemplateUpdate

CredentialUpsertIn.description:
PUT body for one (provider, mode) bundle.

All fields optional so the dashboard can rotate a single secret without
re-sending the others. A field that is OMITTED (exclude_unset) is left
untouched; a field sent as null/empty is NOT written (we never clear a
stored secret by accident — clearing is out of scope for this slice).

The provider is taken from the URL path, not the body — these fields are a
superset and field-coherence is enforced against the path provider in
:meth:`_validate_against_path` (called from the handler).
CredentialUpsertIn.title: CredentialUpsertIn

CustomOfferIn.description: PUT body: the full offer. Replaces all existing lines.
CustomOfferIn.title: CustomOfferIn

CustomOfferLineIn.description: A single line in the composed offer.
CustomOfferLineIn.title: CustomOfferLineIn

repos.description: Optional subset of deployable repos; defaults to all.
repos.title: Repos

DomainEligibility.description:
Response of GET /api/operator/email-sender/eligibility.

Fields:
  domain:   the domain that was classified (normalised lowercase).
  path:     'auto' when Landr will configure DNS automatically;
            'manual' when the operator must publish records themselves.
  provider: 'cloudflare' | 'autodns' | 'manual'  — detected DNS provider.
  in_pool:  True/False when the autoDNS pool probe was called; None otherwise.

in_pool.description: autoDNS pool-membership result (True/False/None if not applicable).
in_pool.title: In Pool

DomainEligibility.title: DomainEligibility

configured.description: True iff an operator_email_sender row exists.
configured.title: Configured

dns_records.description: DNS records the operator must publish (from Resend).
dns_records.title: Dns Records

EstimateAppliedRule.description:
One entry of the applied_rules trace, mirroring the dict appended
by ``app/services/pricing._apply_rule``` /
``compute_booking_price``: ``{rule_id, kind, before, after, detail}``
for an applied rule, or ``{rule_id, kind, before, after, skipped,
skipped_reason}`` (no ``detail`` key) for a skipped one.

landr-y3oj.1: the widget's ``EstimateAppliedRule`` TypeScript
interface only declares ``kind`` + optional ``detail`` (missing
``rule_id``/``before``/``after``/``skipped``/``skipped_reason``); the
dashboard's ``SimulateAppliedRule`` declares ``rule_id``/``kind``/
``before``/``after``/``detail`` as all-required (missing the
skipped-branch fields, and ``detail`` is actually absent — not just
empty — when a rule is skipped). See landr-y3oj.1 handoff for this
divergence. ``extra="allow"`` keeps this forward-compatible.
EstimateAppliedRule.title: EstimateAppliedRule

EstimateLineItem.description:
One row in the PriceSidebar breakdown (landr-qez0). Mirrors the
widget's ``EstimateLineItem`` TypeScript interface (src/api/types.ts)
and the dashboard's ``SimulateLineItem`` (src/lib/pricing-simulator.ts)
field-for-field. ``unit_price``/``line_total`` are decimal strings
(e.g. "60.00"), matching ``_compute_line``'s wire format exactly.
EstimateLineItem.title: EstimateLineItem

EstimateResponse.description:
Response of POST …/products/{id}/estimate (landr-xbqh). Mirrors the
widget's ``EstimateResponse`` TypeScript interface (src/api/types.ts)
and the dashboard's ``SimulateEstimateResponse``
(src/lib/pricing-simulator.ts) field-for-field.
EstimateResponse.title: EstimateResponse

FlowFieldDef.description:
One field definition from the form RPC (verbatim wire keys).
Mirrors the widget's ``FlowFieldDef`` TypeScript interface.
FlowFieldDef.title: FlowFieldDef

FlowFieldOption.description:
One option in a select/radio/checkbox/multiselect field. Mirrors
the widget's ``FlowFieldOption`` TypeScript interface
(src/api/flowTypes.ts) — EXCEPT the widget declares ``label`` as a
required string while the actual ``form_fields.options`` jsonb column
has no such constraint: rows with only ``{"value": ...}`` (no
``label`` key) are valid and observed on the wire (see
landr-y3oj.1 handoff for this divergence). Modelled as optional here
to avoid a response-validation 500 on legacy/incomplete option rows —
this is the actual current wire shape, not a behavior change.

``value`` is optional for the same reason: ``form_fields`` jsonb has no
shape CHECK constraint, so a malformed/legacy options entry missing
``value`` is a real state on the wire, not a hypothetical.
FlowFieldOption.title: FlowFieldOption

FlowFieldValidation.description:
Numeric/length/pattern validation blob. Mirrors the widget's
``FlowFieldValidation`` TypeScript interface.
FlowFieldValidation.title: FlowFieldValidation

FlowFormDef.description:
One form embedded in a custom_form flow module. Mirrors the
widget's ``FlowFormDef`` TypeScript interface.
FlowFormDef.title: FlowFormDef

FlowModuleDef.description:
One module from the public_get_product_flow RPC. Mirrors the
widget's ``FlowModuleDef`` TypeScript interface.
FlowModuleDef.title: FlowModuleDef

FormResponseIn.description:
landr-71kz.2: one custom form's answers in a booking submit payload.

``form_key`` references a form attached to the product's flow (a custom_form
module). ``answers`` is a flat {field_key: value} map; the server re-evaluates
each field's visibility_rule and validates required-when-visible / type /
options / validation before persisting a booking_form_responses row.
FormResponseIn.title: FormResponseIn

GroupInquiryRequest.description:
Body for the public larger-group inquiry form.

Minimal-but-safe anti-abuse for an anon surface: bounded string lengths,
a freeform email-format check, and party_size clamped to 1..99.

Only name and email are required (landr-vlxm: message is now optional so
widget submissions without a message don't 422; phone is a new optional
field threaded through to the email template).
GroupInquiryRequest.title: GroupInquiryRequest

MarkTicketsShippedIn.description:
Request body for mark_tickets_shipped.

ids:         1–100 ticket UUIDs to mark as done.
release_ref: Git SHA, tag, or freeform deploy identifier stored in the
             ``ticket.shipped`` event payload for traceability.
MarkTicketsShippedIn.title: MarkTicketsShippedIn

NotifyMentionsIn.description:
Body for the notify-mentions endpoint.

comment_id:          The UUID of the comment that was just posted.
mentioned_user_ids:  public.users.id list of users explicitly @mentioned in
                     the comment body (already resolved by the client).
body:                A short excerpt of the comment body — included as the
                     bell notification body so recipients see context.
NotifyMentionsIn.title: NotifyMentionsIn

NotifyMentionsOut.description: Dispatch summary returned to the caller.
NotifyMentionsOut.title: NotifyMentionsOut

OfferCreateIn.description: POST body. title is required; the rest are optional.
OfferCreateIn.title: OfferCreateIn

OfferCustomer.description:
``customer`` block of the public_get_booking_by_token RPC. Mirrors
the widget's ``OfferCustomer`` TypeScript interface.
OfferCustomer.title: OfferCustomer

OfferParticipant.description:
One entry of the RPC's ``participants`` array. Mirrors the widget's
``OfferParticipant`` TypeScript interface.
OfferParticipant.title: OfferParticipant

OfferPatchIn.description: PATCH body. All fields optional; only those set are written.
OfferPatchIn.title: OfferPatchIn

OfferProductLine.description:
One entry of the RPC's ``product_lines`` array. Mirrors the
widget's ``OfferProductLine`` TypeScript interface.
OfferProductLine.title: OfferProductLine

OfferTotals.description:
``totals`` block of the public_get_booking_by_token RPC (migration
20260512221007). Mirrors the widget's ``OfferTotals`` TypeScript
interface (src/api/client.ts) field-for-field.
OfferTotals.title: OfferTotals

OperatorPatch.description: Partial update payload — every field is optional.
OperatorPatch.title: OperatorPatch

OperatorProduct.description:
Public catalogue product row.

Mirrors the public_get_operator_products RPC projection, with the image
PATHS already composed into URLs by the router. `model_config = "allow"`
keeps the model forward-compatible: RPC columns the widget doesn't yet
consume (and new ones future migrations add) pass through untouched
rather than forcing a response-model bump on every RPC change.
OperatorProduct.title: OperatorProduct

OperatorProductGroup.description:
Public category row for the widget's category-first entrance.

product_count is the number of currently-listable products in the group's
descendant subtree (the widget hides product_count=0 groups itself).
OperatorProductGroup.title: OperatorProductGroup

OperatorSettings.description:
Operator-level rendering/behaviour flags for the public booking widget.

Returned by GET /api/public/operators/{token}/settings. Fields mirror the
public_get_operator_settings RPC projection exactly; extra=allow keeps the
model forward-compatible so new RPC columns pass through without forcing a
response-model bump on every migration.

landr-jb1k: widget_variant and widget_category_columns added.
OperatorSettings.title: OperatorSettings

PatchMoscowIn.description:
Body for the moscow PATCH endpoint.

moscow: The MoSCoW category to assign, or null to clear it.
    Allowed values: 'must' | 'should' | 'could' | 'wont' | null.
PatchMoscowIn.title: PatchMoscowIn

PendingDecisionDetail.description: Full detail including landr_snapshot and candidates jsonb.
PendingDecisionDetail.title: PendingDecisionDetail

PendingDecisionSummary.description: Paginated-list item: row fields + denormalised booking summary.
PendingDecisionSummary.title: PendingDecisionSummary

PreviewMigrationsResponse.description:
Read-only preview shown in the propose/promote dialogs (landr-a99u.14.6).

``pending_count`` is the number of migrations the target tier would receive
if a run of ``kind`` were started right now; ``files`` is the ordered list
of filenames (ascending timestamp), matching what the executor would apply.
PreviewMigrationsResponse.title: PreviewMigrationsResponse

ProductAddon.description:
One row returned by the public_get_product_addons RPC (landr-cip6 /
epic landr-ie8g). Mirrors the widget's ``ProductAddon`` TypeScript
interface (src/api/types.ts) field-for-field.
ProductAddon.title: ProductAddon

ProductFlowResponse.description:
Response of GET …/products/{id}/flow. Mirrors the widget's
``ProductFlowResponse`` TypeScript interface (src/api/flowTypes.ts).
``modules`` is null for products with no configured flow (legacy
shape) or list of modules otherwise.
ProductFlowResponse.title: ProductFlowResponse

parent_id.description: New parent group id, or null to move to the tree root.
parent_id.title: Parent Id

ProductImage.description: One image rendition pair for a product gallery (epic landr-d8rg).
ProductImage.title: ProductImage

PromoteTicketIn.description:
Body for the promote endpoint.

promotion_prompt: Olaf's engineering prompt — the human-compiled
    description of the customer problem translated into engineering work.
    This is what the bridge worker (.19) will pass to `bd create`.
    Must be non-empty (min_length=1); strip-only content is also rejected
    by the router before writing.
PromoteTicketIn.title: PromoteTicketIn

PublicBookingOffer.description:
Full response of GET /api/public/bookings/{token}.

Mirrors the public_get_booking_by_token RPC jsonb shape (migration
20260512221007) plus the router-injected ``ical_url`` (landr-3vr5).
Mirrors the widget's ``PublicBookingOffer`` TypeScript interface
(src/api/client.ts) — EXCEPT the widget's interface does not declare
``ical_url`` even though the router has injected it since landr-3vr5;
see landr-y3oj.1 handoff for this divergence. ``extra="allow"`` keeps
this forward-compatible with RPC columns not yet consumed here.
PublicBookingOffer.title: PublicBookingOffer

RelayTicketFields.description: The relayable subset of a staging ticket. title + type are required.
RelayTicketFields.title: RelayTicketFields

ResendEmailBody.description: Optional overrides for the resent email.  All default to the source row.
ResendEmailBody.title: ResendEmailBody

ResendVerificationRequest.description:
POST /api/public/signup/resend-verification body.

Only the email is required — the caller (marketing site success state) does
NOT send a Turnstile token on resend. Rate limiting (per-IP + global) is the
sole abuse control.
ResendVerificationRequest.title: ResendVerificationRequest

RetrievePatchIn.description:
PATCH body — operator sets the retrieve workflow state/note.

Both optional; only fields explicitly set are written. retrieve_state is
free text by design (operator-defined pickup workflow); the customer-set
fields (status/coords/note) are NOT settable here.
RetrievePatchIn.title: RetrievePatchIn

SavedViewIn.description: Create payload. creator_user_id is server-stamped from the JWT.
SavedViewIn.title: SavedViewIn

SavedViewPatch.description:
Partial update — every field optional. entity_type is immutable
(changing a View's entity_type would invalidate its config completely).
SavedViewPatch.title: SavedViewPatch

recipient_id.description: Internal users.id (NOT supabase_auth_id) of the recipient.
recipient_id.title: Recipient Id

ServiceRoleIn.description: Create payload for a new service role.
ServiceRoleIn.title: ServiceRoleIn

ServiceRolePatch.description:
Partial update — every field optional. `code` is intentionally
excluded: the widget's submitted `service_role_code` and any
booking_participants already pointing at this role would break if the
code changed under them. Rename via `label`, not `code`.
ServiceRolePatch.title: ServiceRolePatch

from_local_part.description: Local part of the From address; defaults to 'bookings'.
from_local_part.title: From Local Part

sending_domain.description: Root domain to send from, e.g. acme.com.
sending_domain.title: Sending Domain

SignupRequest.description:
POST /api/public/signup body.

`name` is the operator/business display name (-> operators.name). `slug` is
the desired public handle. `turnstile_token` is the Cloudflare widget's
client response token. `password` is the new account password (GoTrue
enforces its own minimum; we keep a soft floor here). `locale` selects the
verification-email language (en/de; defaults en).

Both field names and their marketing-site aliases are accepted (back-compat):
  name / operator_name, slug / operator_slug  (landr-oqrz.1)
SignupRequest.title: SignupRequest

push_delivery.default: stubbed — real push lands with landr-71a2
push_delivery.title: Push Delivery

StagingActivityIn.description:
A cross-tier activity event for a relayed staging ticket.

One of ``source_ref`` (preferred — the stable staging ticket id the .1 relay
carried) or ``ticket_id`` (the prod tickets.id of the relayed row) MUST be
present. ``reporter_email`` is the staging reporter, matched BY EMAIL against
prod public.users.
StagingActivityIn.title: StagingActivityIn

SubmitBookingResponse.description:
Response of POST /api/public/bookings (booking submit).

Mirrors public_submit_booking's jsonb response (booking_id,
semantic_state, stage_code, approval_outcome, next_steps) plus the
fields this router injects afterwards (confirmation_email_status,
ical_url, calendar_event). Mirrors the widget's
``SubmitBookingResponse`` TypeScript interface (src/api/types.ts)
field-for-field — including its optional ``token`` field, which is
never actually populated by the current RPC (see landr-y3oj.1
handoff for this divergence). ``extra="allow"`` keeps this
forward-compatible with RPC columns not yet consumed here.
SubmitBookingResponse.title: SubmitBookingResponse

TagAssignmentIn.description: Full-replace set of tag ids for one booking or contact.
TagAssignmentIn.title: TagAssignmentIn

TagIn.description: Create payload for a new operator tag.
TagIn.title: TagIn

to.description: Recipient email address for the test message.
to.title: To

TestEmailResult.description:
Result of POST /api/operator/email-sender/test.

Always 200 — the caller distinguishes success from failure via ``status``.
This lets the dashboard render a persistent log line for both outcomes.
TestEmailResult.title: TestEmailResult

ViewReorderItem.description:
One element of the bulk reorder payload — a (view_id, sort_order)
pair scoped to the caller's view_user_state rows.
ViewReorderItem.title: ViewReorderItem

ViewUserStateIn.description:
Per-user state patch. All fields optional — any combination is allowed
in a single request. `pinned` replaces the historic `starred` flag
(landr-45pb). `sort_order` is the per-user DnD-persisted index within
the Primary (pinned) bucket; it has no meaning when pinned=false but
is preserved across pin toggles so a re-pin restores the prior slot.
ViewUserStateIn.title: ViewUserStateIn

VisibilityRule.description:
Single-rule v1 visibility rule (contract-pinned). Mirrors the
widget's ``VisibilityRule`` TypeScript interface — EXCEPT ``field_key``
and ``op`` are optional here even though the widget/contract declare
them required: ``form_fields`` jsonb has no shape CHECK, and
``app/services/form_responses.is_field_visible`` explicitly treats a
rule missing either as malformed and fail-opens (always visible)
rather than erroring. A required str here would 500 the response on
exactly the row shape the evaluator is written to tolerate.
VisibilityRule.title: VisibilityRule

VoucherIn.description: Create payload for a new operator voucher.
VoucherIn.title: VoucherIn

VoucherPatch.description: Partial update — every field optional. used_count is not editable.
VoucherPatch.title: VoucherPatch

post.description:
Record (or refresh) a legal disclaimer acknowledgment for the current user.

Idempotent: upserts on (user_id, ack_type). Returns 201 always.

get.description: Check whether the current user has acknowledged the legal disclaimer.

Get all expiry / service-due alerts for the current user.

Reads member_expiry_alerts view filtered by user_id.
Results include expired/overdue items plus upcoming warnings.
List all gear items for the current user.

post.description: Add a gear item. Requires prior legal disclaimer ack.

get.description: List all gear types (world-readable reference data).

delete.description: Delete a gear item owned by the current user.

patch.description: Update a gear item owned by the current user.

get.description:
List all insurance records for the current user.

Note: policy_number is not returned (encrypted at rest, never exposed via API).

post.description:
Add an insurance record. Requires prior legal disclaimer ack.

If policy_number is provided it is encrypted before storage.

delete.description: Delete an insurance record owned by the current user.

patch.description: Update an insurance record owned by the current user.

get.description: List all license / certification types (world-readable reference data).
List all license records for the current user.

post.description: Add a new license record. Requires prior legal disclaimer ack.

delete.description: Delete a license record owned by the current user.

patch.description: Update a license record owned by the current user.

post.description: Send a direct message after permission + rate-limit checks.

Upload a community photo (site or gear).

Quota is checked before insert. Site photos (subject_type='site') are
uncounted. Gear photos count against the user's personal+gear quota.
Returns 409 when the quota is exceeded.

get.description:
Return a short-lived signed URL for a private community-media object.

The community-media bucket is PRIVATE (landr-w6gi.13) — raw object URLs are
not public. Any authenticated community member may read a non-removed asset
(mirrors the media_assets SELECT RLS policy: moderation_status != 'removed').
'removed' assets return 410 so a takedown actually hides the bytes.

media_id is the media_assets.id.

post.description:
Flag a photo for moderation review.

Any authenticated community member may flag. Sets moderation_status →
'flagged'. Staff action is out-of-band (admin queue, not this API).

photo_id is the media_assets.id (not the link table id).

get.description: Return today + upcoming cached flyability forecasts for an activity_site.

post.description:
Raise an emergency SOS and compute the broadcast recipient set.

Push delivery is stubbed in v1. The response includes `broadcast_recipients`
(who WOULD be notified) so the mobile UI can display a "help is on the way"
count immediately. Real push delivery lands with landr-71a2.

Deploy-close: set status='done' and emit ticket.shipped for each ticket.

Called by CI pipelines after a successful rollout on any pipeline
(Cloud Run, EAS, Cloudflare Pages). Generic by design.

Auth:
    X-Deploy-Token header (CI path) OR landr-staff JWT (manual path).
    Returns 401 when neither is supplied; 403 when credentials are invalid.

Idempotency:
    Already-done tickets → skipped_done (no DB write, no notification).
    Missing tickets → skipped_not_found (skipped silently).

Returns:
    200 with shipped / skipped_done / skipped_not_found lists.
post.summary: Set status=done + emit ticket.shipped for each ticket UUID (deploy-close).

post.description:
Idempotently upsert a relayed staging ticket into landr (prod).

Auth: X-Feedback-Relay-Token (service-to-service, fail-closed).

Behaviour (idempotent, keyed on source_ref):
  * if a relayed row with this source_ref exists → UPDATE it ("updated").
  * else INSERT a new staging-origin row ("created").
The row is stamped origin_tier='staging', operator_id NULL,
origin_operator_label set. Comments are appended (monotonic reconcile on
re-relay).

Returns {"status": "created"|"updated", "ticket_id": ..., "comments_synced": n}.
post.summary: Ingest a relayed STAGING ticket (+ comments) into the PROD inbox (service-to-service).

post.description:
Resolve the customer's prod identity by email and (if opted in) notify them.

Auth: X-Feedback-Relay-Token (service-to-service, fail-closed).

Behaviour:
  * Resolve the relayed staging-origin prod ticket (source_ref or ticket_id).
  * Resolve the prod user BY EMAIL.
  * Gate on users.notify_staging_in_prod (default ON).
  * If ON, dispatch a bell (+ push/email per the user's channels) via the
    existing NotificationDispatcher, targeting that user directly.

Returns {"outcome": ..., "matched_user_id": ..., "bell_user_ids": [...]}.
Outcomes other than "dispatched" (no_match / no_ticket / suppressed_pref)
are still 200 — they are expected no-ops, not errors.
post.summary: Echo a relayed STAGING ticket's activity to the reporter's PROD identity (matched by email, gated on notify_staging_in_prod) — service-to-service.

post.description:
Create (or dedupe) a customer-originated staging→main proposal.

Auth: X-Release-Relay-Token (service-to-service). Behaviour:
  * DEDUPE — if an open staging_to_main run (proposed, signoff_source=
    'customer') already exists, refresh it and return already_pending.
  * else create a proposed staging_to_main run (signoff_source='customer',
    signoff_by_label=signer_label), pinning current staging SHAs, then
    NOTIFY the landr staff approvers (same path as the staff propose flow).

Returns {"status": "requested" | "already_pending", "run_id": ...}.

LEGACY app-level endpoint — verifies the app-level secret only.

Behaviour UNCHANGED by landr-1nwu.3 (platform / single-tenant back-compat).

get.description:
Return the caller's resolved staff capability block (staff-only).

403 (landr_staff_required) if the caller maps to no public.users row or
resolves to a non-staff account (no umbrella flag and no role facet).
Run history, newest-first.

post.description:
Promote dev→staging. Promoter|approver. Creates a queued run.

Tier gate (landr-7dya.21): only the DEV api may create dev_to_staging
runs. Staging + prod 403 with ``wrong_tier_for_promotion``.

get.description:
Local working-tree status of the deployable repos — DEV/Trillian ONLY.

The env matrix (``/status``) reports against GitHub branch tips, so it shows
a repo "up to date" even when Trillian's local checkout has uncommitted or
unpushed work. This surfaces that gap: per repo, current branch +
uncommitted/untracked counts + ahead/behind vs ``origin/<branch>`` (read
from LOCAL refs — no fetch).

HARD GATING (landr local-worktree): this NEVER runs on Cloud Run. It is
enabled only when ``LANDR_DEV_LOCAL_WORKTREE=1`` is set AND the repos base
dir exists on disk. When not enabled (every staging/prod service) it returns
200 ``{"enabled": false, "repos": []}`` so the dashboard hides the section
cleanly — no 404 noise, no error toast. Still staff-gated: a non-staff
bearer gets the usual 403 even on dev.

``stale`` flags that ahead/behind come from local refs without a fetch, so
the numbers may lag the true remote state.

List the migrations that would be applied to the target tier if a run of
``kind`` were started right now.

Read-only — no side effects, no DB writes. Used by the dashboard
propose/promote dialogs (landr-a99u.14.6) to surface "will apply N
migrations" before the run is created. NOT a gate — informational only.

Errors (mirrored back as 503/502 so the dashboard can render a warning
rather than crash):

- 503 ``migration_target_not_configured`` — the env var holding the target
  tier's DB URL (``LANDR_STAGING_DB_URL`` / ``LANDR_PROD_DB_URL``,
  provisioned via landr-a99u.14.5) is unset on the running api. The
  promotion executor will surface the same gap as a hard error.
- 502 ``migration_target_unreachable: <type>: <msg>`` — the connection
  attempt against the target Supabase failed (auth, network, etc).

post.description:
Propose staging→main. Promoter|approver. Creates a proposed run; notifies approvers.

Tier gate (landr-7dya.21): only the STAGING api may create
staging_to_main runs. Dev + prod 403 with ``wrong_tier_for_promotion``.
Decisions (approve / reject / cancel) are likewise staging-only.

get.description:
Env matrix across the deployable repos + the viewer capability block.

The env matrix is per-repo dev/staging/main SHAs and ahead_by for
dev→staging and staging→main (via the GitHub compare API). The viewer
block tells the dashboard which buttons to enable — gating on capabilities,
not raw roles.

``tier`` is the current api's deploy tier (development | staging |
production) — the UI uses it to decide which action set to render
(landr-7dya.21): dev shows "Promote to staging", staging shows
"Request go-live" / "Approve & promote", prod shows neither.
Run detail: the run plus its per-repo checklist rows.

post.description:
Approve a proposed staging→main run. Approver only. proposed→queued.

Tier gate (landr-7dya.21): approve is a staging_to_main-only operation
(dev_to_staging never enters the ``proposed`` state). Only the STAGING
api may execute decisions; dev + prod 403 with ``wrong_tier_for_promotion``.

Cancel one's own proposed staging→main run. Proposer only. proposed→cancelled.

Tier gate (landr-7dya.21): staging-only, same shape as ``approve``.

Reject a proposed staging→main run. Approver only. proposed→rejected.

Tier gate (landr-7dya.21): staging-only, same shape as ``approve``.

get.description:
Platform-commission revenue overview, per operator + overall.

LANDR-STAFF ONLY. Optional `operator_id` narrows to a single operator;
otherwise every operator that has an active PLATFORM commission scheme is
included.

post.description:
Insert a bd_intent row (status=pending) for the given ticket.

The Trillian-side bd_intent_drain worker picks this up on its next poll,
runs ``bd create``, and writes back ``bd_intent.bd_id``,
``bd_intent.status=done``, and ``tickets.linked_bd_id``. If the drain
worker is offline (Trillian down), the row stays pending and settles
when the drain comes back online.

Auth:
    Returns 403 for any caller without is_landr_staff = true.

Returns:
    201 with the newly created bd_intent row.
post.summary: Queue a bd_intent outbox row to file a bd issue for a ticket (landr-staff only).

patch.description:
Set or clear the MoSCoW tag for release scoping (landr-staff only).

Passing null clears the tag (ticket no longer planned for the current
milestone). The moscow column has a SELECT grant for authenticated so
operators can see it, but no INSERT/UPDATE grant — this service-role
endpoint is the only write path.

Auth:
    Returns 403 for any caller without is_landr_staff = true.

Returns:
    200 with id and the updated moscow value (may be null).
patch.summary: Set or clear the MoSCoW release-planning tag on a ticket (landr-staff only).

post.description:
Write Olaf's engineering prompt and stamp promotion_requested_at on a ticket.

This is the intake that the Trillian bridge worker (landr-wwhn.19)
watches. The bridge worker polls/subscribes for tickets where
promotion_requested_at IS NOT NULL AND linked_bd_id IS NULL.

Idempotency:
    Calling this endpoint again UPDATES the prompt and resets
    promotion_requested_at. This allows Olaf to refine the prompt
    before the bridge has consumed it. If the bridge has already
    processed the ticket (linked_bd_id is set), the prompt is still
    updated but the reset promotion_requested_at will cause the bridge
    worker to re-check — the bridge MUST handle the already-linked case
    idempotently (skip, don't duplicate the bd issue).

Auth:
    Returns 403 for any caller without is_landr_staff = true in
    public.users. Operators cannot reach this endpoint.

Returns:
    200 with the updated ticket fields (id, promotion_prompt,
    promotion_requested_at, linked_bd_id).
post.summary: Set promotion_prompt + promotion_requested_at on a ticket (landr-staff only).

get.description:
Return the operator's current sending-domain config + status + records.

Lazy reconciliation: while verification is still ``pending`` we re-check SES
on read and persist the result, so the dashboard's existing poll flips the
badge from "verifying" to "verified" on its own — the operator never has to
click "Verify" for an auto-provisioned (records-already-published) domain.
Best-effort: a missing SES client or any SES error leaves the stored status
untouched and never fails the GET.

Classify whether a domain will be set up automatically or requires manual DNS records.

NO SIDE EFFECTS: does not create SES identities, DNS records, or provisioning jobs.
Used by the dashboard 'Check domain' button so operators know the path before
committing to the setup flow.

Returns:
  * ``path='auto'``   — Cloudflare zone found in our account, OR autoDNS NS
                        detected AND the pool probe does NOT return explicit False
                        (i.e. True or None → auto; mirrors the enqueue rule).
  * ``path='manual'`` — non-InterNetX/CF NS, no CF token, or probe returns
                        explicit False (zone confirmed not in our account).

parameters.description: Root domain to classify, e.g. acme.com.
parameters.name: domain

schema.description: Root domain to classify, e.g. acme.com.
schema.title: Domain

post.description:
Create the SES domain identity, store its DKIM records, and provision DNS.

Flow:
  1. Create the SES email identity for ``sending_domain`` (EasyDKIM) and get
     back the 3 DKIM CNAME records.
  2. Persist the DKIM records on ``operator_email_sender`` (status='pending').
  3. Provision DNS by detecting the zone's provider (NS lookup):
     Cloudflare → push records directly (verified path); autoDNS → enqueue a
     Marvin-side job; manual → return the records for the operator to paste.
     Store the detected provider + a human note in ``last_error`` is NOT used
     for the happy path; provider lands in ``dns_provider``.

503 'ses_not_configured' when AWS creds are unset — never a 500.

Send a one-off test email FROM the operator's verified sending domain.

Always returns 200 with a structured result so the dashboard can render a
log line for both success and failure.  Only a 422 is raised for an invalid
``to`` address (Pydantic validation) — all other failure modes return
``{"status": "failed", ...}`` at 200.

Flow:
  1. Load the ``operator_email_sender`` row.  No row or no domain → failed.
  2. Check ``verification_status == 'verified'``.  Otherwise → failed.
  3. Build the SES client.  No credentials → failed.
  4. Compose a short test message from ``{from_local_part}@{sending_domain}``.
  5. Send via SES; surface ``SESError`` (sandbox / unverified recipient /
     throttle) in the detail so the operator sees the exact rejection reason.

Re-check the SES domain verification status.

Reads SES's identity/DKIM attrs for the operator's sending domain and
mirrors the result onto ``operator_email_sender.verification_status``
(``verified`` clears ``last_error``). 503 'ses_not_configured' when AWS
creds are unset.

Reconciliation: before polling SES, checks the latest dns_provisioning_jobs
row for the operator's domain. If the drainer marked the job 'failed', OR
the job has been 'pending' longer than AUTODNS_JOB_PENDING_TIMEOUT_MINUTES,
we degrade dns_provider to 'manual' with a clear last_error and return the
current row — the operator gets the copy-paste records instead of spinning
forever. Existing dns_records are kept intact so the copy-paste table is
available. If the job is 'applied', we proceed with the normal SES check.

get.description:
Whether the caller may file a customer request-go-live.

True iff the caller carries ``is_release_signer`` AND this deployment is the
staging/relay side (non-production + control-plane URL configured). On the
control plane itself, in production, or for a non-signer, returns False.

post.description:
File a customer request-go-live and relay it to the dev control plane.

Requires ``is_release_signer`` and the staging/relay deployment side. Relays
over the shared service secret to
POST {control_plane}/api/internal/release/customer-signoff and mirrors back
the control-plane status (``requested`` | ``already_pending``).

Resend an existing outbound email, optionally overriding recipient/subject/body.

Creates a NEW outbound_emails row (audit trail preserved) and drains it
inline.  Returns the new row id, final status, and transport used.

get.description:
Paginated list of unresolved Holded contact decisions for an operator.

Ordered by created_at ASC (FIFO). Includes a short booking summary
denormalised from external_sync_log + bookings.
Full detail for a single pending decision, including landr_snapshot and candidates.

post.description:
Resolve an ambiguous Holded contact decision.

- action='link': link the booking contact to an existing Holded contact
  (external_id required).
- action='create': create a new Holded contact on the next retry cycle
  (external_id omitted).

Side effects:
  1. Updates pending_external_contact_decisions: resolved_at, resolved_by,
     resolution.
  2. Updates external_sync_log: status='pending', next_retry_at=now() so
     the worker retries on its next cycle.

Returns 409 if already resolved.
Returns 403 if decision belongs to a different operator (belt-and-suspenders;
the auth guard already blocks cross-operator requests by membership check).

get.description:
The group's current check-in statuses (the customer's own view).

Returns the latest check-in per participant for this booking, with the
participant's first name and any operator-set retrieve_state so a flyer can
see "everyone's down, Tom's pickup is on its way". No price fields.

parameters.description: Per-operator long-lived token from operator_ical_tokens. Issued + rotated via the staff endpoints under /api/staff/operators/{operator_id}/ical-token.
parameters.name: token

schema.description: Per-operator long-lived token from operator_ical_tokens. Issued + rotated via the staff endpoints under /api/staff/operators/{operator_id}/ical-token.
schema.title: Token

post.description:
landr-16u9: passwordless LANDR account-link for the post-booking
'Track this booking in the LANDR app' prompt.

Mints a magic link via Supabase admin ``generate_link`` (Supabase sends
NO email) and delivers it through the operator's configured sending domain
(or platform fallback) with our branded ``account_link`` template — so it
never depends on a Supabase SMTP config. Gated on the operator's
``offer_account_link`` opt-in (landr-atwy): when off, returns 404 so the
surface stays opaque.

Email the operator's public_contact_email with a larger-group
inquiry from the booking widget (landr-ehye).

Returns 200 {"ok": true} once the email is queued. When the operator has
NOT set a public_contact_email, returns 422 with a clear detail so the
widget falls back to its bundled mailto. 404 on an unknown widget_token
(enumeration-closed, like every other public operator endpoint).

Rate-limited (landr-v5mq): max 5 inquiries/hour per token+IP, and
20 inquiries/hour per token across all IPs. Returns 429 when exceeded.
The counter is DB-backed (inquiry_rate_limits table) so it works correctly
across multiple GCP Cloud Run instances.

get.description:
Operator's product_groups for the widget's category-first entrance
(epic landr-d8rg, contract item [E]).

Returns every ACTIVE, non-deleted group: id, slug, name, name_localized,
description, description_localized, image_url, sort_order, parent_id, and a
`product_count` = the number of currently-listable products in the group's
descendant subtree (the recursive parent_id walk lives in the RPC). EMPTY
groups (product_count=0) are RETURNED — hiding them is the widget's job, so
deep links into a now-empty category still resolve. Ordered by
(sort_order, name). 404 only on an unknown widget_token.

Operator catalog for the public booking widget.

The widget shortcode `[landr_booking group=...]` forwards `group`
as `?group=<slug>` so each shortcode embed scopes to one
product_groups subtree (landr-up1b; supersedes the flat
`type=`/`group=` exact-match behaviour from landr-m05.20).

Implementation note (landr-up1b): the descendant resolution now
happens INSIDE the SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Since product_groups gained
a self-referential `parent_id` (nested categories), an exact-slug
Python post-filter would miss products filed under child categories.
The RPC takes an optional `group_slug` argument and walks the
subtree with a recursive CTE, so filtering by a parent category also
surfaces every descendant's products. The HTTP contract is
unchanged: callers still pass `?group=<slug>`.

parameters.description: Optional product_groups.slug filter (e.g. 'courses', 'specialty', 'guided-days'). When the slug is a nested category, the filter resolves to that node AND all of its descendants (recursive over product_groups.parent_id). Returns an empty list when no products match the group; only an unknown operator slug yields 404.
parameters.name: group

schema.description: Optional product_groups.slug filter (e.g. 'courses', 'specialty', 'guided-days'). When the slug is a nested category, the filter resolves to that node AND all of its descendants (recursive over product_groups.parent_id). Returns an empty list when no products match the group; only an unknown operator slug yields 404.
schema.title: Group

parameters.description: OPERATOR PREVIEW gate (bd landr-7zc5.1). When present AND equal to this operator's widget_preview_token (a distinct, dashboard-only secret — NOT the widget_token), the catalogue ALSO includes DRAFT products (is_publicly_listed=false) and each row carries an is_publicly_listed flag so the widget can render a DRAFT badge. Absent or non-matching => the live, published-only catalogue (drafts hidden). The preview_token is never embedded in the public live snippet, so live-widget visitors (who hold the widget_token) still cannot see drafts.
parameters.name: preview_token

schema.description: OPERATOR PREVIEW gate (bd landr-7zc5.1). When present AND equal to this operator's widget_preview_token (a distinct, dashboard-only secret — NOT the widget_token), the catalogue ALSO includes DRAFT products (is_publicly_listed=false) and each row carries an is_publicly_listed flag so the widget can render a DRAFT badge. Absent or non-matching => the live, published-only catalogue (drafts hidden). The preview_token is never embedded in the public live snippet, so live-widget visitors (who hold the widget_token) still cannot see drafts.
schema.title: Preview Token

post.description:
Live booking-price estimator for the widget's PriceSidebar.

Reuses the canonical pricing engine (`compute_booking_price`) so the
sidebar preview matches the gross_total persisted by submit_booking
bit-for-bit. No DB writes — pure read + compute.

landr-il9f.1: keyed on the opaque widget_token; an unknown token
(or a real slug passed as the token) 404s before any product lookup.

get.description:
landr-71kz.2 / EPIC A: the product's ordered booking-flow modules.

Thin FastAPI wrapper over the SECURITY DEFINER RPC
``public_get_product_flow``. The widget calls this after the customer picks a
product to learn which modules (selection, participants, accommodation,
custom_form, …) to render in which order, with the attached form definition
(key/version/name + ordered fields) inlined for custom_form modules.

Returns ``{"modules": null}`` when the product has no configured flow — the
LEGACY path the widget reproduces bit-for-bit (zero config = today's
hardcoded flow). Keyed on the opaque widget_token (resolved to the slug
here); an unknown token 404s via ``_resolve_operator_by_token``.

Operator's active participant service_roles (landr-mg0a).

Thin wrapper over the SECURITY DEFINER RPC
`public_get_operator_service_roles`. The widget fetches this once on
App mount and uses the first row as the default service_role_code
when submitting a booking. When the operator has >1 active role the
widget renders a dropdown per participant in DetailsStep so the
customer can pick (e.g. 'pilot' vs 'passenger' for tandem flights).

A 404 fires only when the widget_token is unknown. An operator with
zero active roles returns an empty list — but that shouldn't happen
in practice because the
seed_default_service_role_on_operator_insert trigger (also
landr-mg0a) plants a 'participant' row at operator creation.

Operator-level rendering/behaviour flags for the public widget.

Surfaces small, widget-relevant flags the widget needs on mount:
`expose_seats_to_customer` (the urgency lever from landr-e10.9) plus
the branding fields `logo_url`, `primary_color`, and the human
`name` (landr-yp8x — widget header rendering), plus
`offer_account_link` (landr-atwy — per-operator opt-in for the
post-booking account-link prompt). Future flags slot into the same
row rather than new endpoints. The widget caches this in its
OperatorContext.

Add-ons configured for a parent product (landr-cip6 / epic landr-ie8g).

Thin FastAPI wrapper over the SECURITY DEFINER RPC
`public_get_product_addons`. Returns one row per linked add-on
product, including the add-on's display name, per-unit price (when
the scheme exposes a per_day_base rule), and the product_addons
config (is_required, min_qty, max_qty, sort_order). The widget
calls this after the customer picks a parent product to render
qty steppers + the orange overbook warning + required-min gating.

Returns an empty list when the parent has no add-ons (including
when the parent itself is hidden — see RPC docstring); the widget
treats an empty list as "no add-ons" and skips the add-on UI.

post.description:
Self-onboarding signup pipeline (see module docstring for the full flow).

Success: 201 {"status": "verification_email_sent", "verification_required":
true} — IDENTICAL on both fresh signup AND duplicate email (M1). The
created operator is FREE tier; NO session is returned — the user must click
the verification link we emailed before they can sign in.
operator_id is intentionally omitted here; callers must fetch it AFTER the
user verifies their email (avoids email-enumeration oracle).

Error contract:
  * 403 captcha_*   — Turnstile failed / unavailable.
  * 429             — per-IP or global rate limit exceeded.
  * 503             — rate-limiter infra error (fail CLOSED).
  * 422 invalid_slug — slug fails the grammar/reserved rules (+suggestions).
  * 409 slug_taken   — slug collision (+3 suggested alternates).
  * 500             — GoTrue create failed, OR the RPC failed AFTER the auth
                      user was created (the auth user is then COMPENSATINGLY
                      deleted before responding), OR the verification email
                      could not even be ENQUEUED (also compensated). NOTE:
                      the response 201s as soon as the email is queued —
                      it no longer waits for the actual Resend/SES send
                      (landr-csot; see module docstring).

NOTE (M1): an already-registered EMAIL does NOT yield a distinct error — it
returns the same generic "verification_email_sent" body as a new signup, so
the endpoint can't be used to enumerate which emails have accounts.

Re-send the verification email for an UNCONFIRMED signup.

The marketing site success state calls this when the user clicks
"Resend email" — no Turnstile token is sent; rate limiting (per-IP + global)
is the sole abuse control.

ANTI-ENUMERATION: always returns the same generic
{"status": "verification_email_sent", "verification_required": true} body
regardless of whether the email is unknown, already confirmed, or
unconfirmed — never reveals account state. A verification email is ONLY
sent for a genuinely unconfirmed user; all other cases silently return the
same 200 so callers cannot distinguish them.

Flow for an unconfirmed user:
  1. Look up supabase_auth_id from public.users by email.
  2. Fetch the GoTrue user record and check email_confirmed_at.
  3. If unconfirmed, call admin.generate_link(type="magiclink") to mint a
     fresh one-time confirmation link without requiring the user's password.
     (A magiclink confirms the email on first use and signs the user in —
     identical end-state to the original signup confirmation link.)
  4. Resolve the operator display name (users.id -> operator_memberships ->
     operators.name) so the signup_verify template's {{ operator_name }}
     has a value (StrictUndefined requires the key to exist).
  5. Send the signup_verify template via send_system_email exactly as the
     main signup path does.

get.description:
Debounced client check: is this slug available?

Returns {"available": bool}. When unavailable (taken, reserved, or
malformed) also returns {"suggestions": [...]} with up to 3 alternates so
the form can offer one-tap fixes. Never 404s — an invalid slug is simply
"available": false. Anonymous; no captcha. Lightly per-IP rate-limited
(DoS guard) since it does DB work for an anonymous caller.
Cheap liveness probe; no auth required.

delete.description:
Soft-cancel a booking. Reason is required.

Sets bookings.deleted_at + deletion_reason; the capacity trigger
releases reserved seats automatically. The booking row remains
for audit / history purposes (and stays visible via include_deleted
filters in future list endpoints).

patch.description:
Edit booking-level fields (currently: customer contact reassign).

Per-line-item edits (selected_days, date_range) go through
PATCH /{booking_id}/products/{booking_product_id} because they
trigger a pricing recompute.

post.description:
Apply a staff approval transition (general/secondary, approve/reject).

Wraps the SECURITY DEFINER RPC transition_booking_approval. The RPC
is the authoritative gate; this route adds:
  - Pre-validation of the booking's current_stage_code for a clean
    409 (instead of an opaque RPC error).
  - Response payload composed from the RPC return + a re-fetch so
    the dashboard can update without a second round-trip.

patch.description:
Edit a booking line item.

If selected_days or date_range changes, re-runs the pricing engine
and updates computed_price_breakdown + rolls up booking totals.

get.description:
List agent_earnings rows for the operator, newest first.

Optional filters: agent_user_id, status_filter (accrued|paid|reversed).
Read-only — earnings accrue via triggers/jobs, not this API.

Per-agent accrued/paid/reversed commission totals for the operator.

Aggregates agent_earnings.commission_amount grouped by agent_user_id
and status. Returns one row per agent with the three status buckets
(+ currency, which is uniform per agent in practice). Read-only.

Done in-Python over the operator's earning rows rather than via a
SQL GROUP BY because the supabase REST client doesn't expose
aggregate grouping; the row volume per operator is small (one row
per agent×booking×day) and bounded by the same 1000-row guard.

post.description:
Send a booking_reminder email for each booking_id in the body.

Behaviour:
  * Validates every booking_id belongs to ``operator_id`` via a
    single SELECT — cross-tenant ids land in ``failed`` without
    any email enqueue attempt.
  * For each in-tenant booking, calls
    :func:`send_booking_reminder` which is best-effort (returns
    ``None`` on enqueue / template / loader failure and logs at
    ERROR). A ``None`` return → the booking_id goes into
    ``failed``.
  * Empty list is rejected by Pydantic with 422 (min_length=1) —
    callers should not POST nothing.

Kill the current public link and mint a new one.

Use when a link has been over-shared (forwarded into a big group chat).
The old URL immediately 404s; the operator re-sends the new one.

get.description:
Return the last-sent timestamp + material-change flag for the dashboard button.

has_material_changes is True when the current booking state differs
materially from what was in the last confirmation email.

Render and stream the invoice PDF for ``booking_id``.

Returns ``application/pdf`` with ``Content-Disposition: attachment``
so the browser triggers a download (the dashboard fetches with auth
headers then uses ``createObjectURL`` to surface the file).

Raises 403 when the operator's ``booking_invoice_download`` feature is
disabled (landr-xfcy). Raises 404 for cross-tenant / unknown booking.
List all notes on one booking, newest first.

post.description:
Create a new note on a booking.

operator_id is stamped from the path (verified to match the booking
via _ensure_booking_belongs_to_operator). author_user_id is taken
from the resolved staff membership — never from the request body, so
callers can't forge authorship.

A whitespace-only payload is rejected by Pydantic (min_length=1)
AND by the schema CHECK constraint as a second line of defence.

delete.description:
Hard-delete one note.

v1 hard-deletes — operators jot quick context and may want to remove a
typo'd note. audit_trigger captures the deleted row so the audit log
still has the full history; the booking_notes table itself just stays
tidy. The three .eq() filters scope the delete to (operator, booking,
note) so a cross-operator note_id can't be wiped through a wrong-
operator path param.

post.description:
Resend booking_confirmation with an old→new diff if material fields changed.

- Loads the current booking bundle.
- Loads last_confirmation_context from the bookings row.
- Diffs material fields.
- Sends booking_confirmation with is_update=True + changes list (even when
  no material changes — the operator explicitly requested a resend).
- Persists the new context snapshot.
- Returns {changes_detected, changes}.

Send the first booking_confirmation to the customer.

- Verifies the booking belongs to this operator.
- Guards: only allowed when the booking is in/past the confirmed set (same
  gate as resend, landr-tf39) AND no prior confirmation exists yet.
  If a prior exists → 409 {"error": "already_confirmed"} (use resend).
  If the stage is not yet confirmed/due → 409 {"error": "confirmation_not_allowed_yet"}.
- Calls send_booking_confirmation(is_update=False, no extra_context) which
  records the initial last_confirmation_context snapshot.
- Returns {"sent": true, "email_id": "<id or null>"}.

Send the custom offer to the booking's customer.

Mints a booking token and delivers the ``custom_offer`` email with a
'Review & Pay' CTA to the widget's /offer/{token} accept page.
Full-replace the tag set on one booking. Returns {tag_ids: [...]}.

get.description:
List an operator's campaigns. Soft-deleted rows are excluded.

Ordered by (sort_order, code) to match the dashboard's manual-order
expectation while keeping a deterministic tiebreak.

post.description:
Create a campaign scoped to operator_id.

operator_id is server-stamped from the path. A duplicate code among
the operator's active campaigns surfaces as 409 (the partial unique
index allows re-using a soft-deleted code).

delete.description:
Soft-delete a campaign (stamp deleted_at + audit fields).

Hard delete is intentionally not exposed: the bookings.campaign_id FK
is ON DELETE SET NULL, so a hard delete would wipe historical
attribution. Soft delete keeps the row and frees the code for reuse
(partial unique index is WHERE deleted_at IS NULL).

patch.description:
Partial update. Empty patch → 400; missing/cross-tenant row → 404.

The update is double-scoped (id + operator_id) and restricted to
non-deleted rows so a cross-operator id or a soft-deleted campaign
lands a clean 404 instead of silently editing nothing.

get.description:
The retrieve board for one operator + one day.

Lists every live check-in, newest activity last, joining the participant's
first name through booking_participants -> contacts so the board reads
"Anna — arrived elsewhere — pin 28.1,-13.5 — pending".

patch.description: Operator sets retrieve_state / retrieve_note on one check-in.

get.description:
Return the operator's checklist template, auto-seeding on first read.

Auto-seed is server-side (here) rather than via an AFTER INSERT
trigger on operators, per memory
`default-product-groups-genericity-trap`: avoid vertical-flavoured
seed triggers on operators.

put.description:
Replace the operator's template wholesale.

Upsert on operator_id — handles both first-time customisation
(no row yet) and ongoing edits in one path.

post.description: Full-replace the tag set on one contact. Returns {tag_ids: [...]}.

get.description:
Return the effective (resolved) template for (operator, kind, locale).

landr-x5o5.4: runs the standard 6-step resolver chain from
:func:`app.services.email_sender.resolve_template` and exposes
the result together with ``is_default`` (True when the resolved
template is a Landr global default — steps 4-6 — rather than an
operator-specific custom row — steps 1-3) and ``source`` (the
chain step that matched).

The dashboard uses this to prefill the editor so the template
editor is never blank even before the operator has saved anything.

Response shape::

    {
      "kind": "booking_confirmation",
      "locale": "en",
      "subject": "...",
      "body_html": "...",
      "body_text": "...",
      "is_default": true,
      "source": "system"
    }

Raises 404 when the resolver chain is exhausted (configuration
error — every supported kind must have at least one code-bundled
fallback).

Return the per-kind variable catalog for the dashboard editor.

landr-x5o5.5: replaces the single flat _PREVIEW_CONTEXT with a
per-kind catalog.  The dashboard fetches this for the selected kind
as soon as the editor loads — no saved template required.

Response shape::

    {
      "kind": "booking_confirmation",
      "variables": [
        {"name": "customer_first_name",
         "sample": "Sample",
         "description": "Customer first name"},
        ...
      ]
    }

post.description:
Render the template against a sample context.

landr-tq6j: uses the same Jinja2 + autoescape engine as the
sender. Operators can validate placeholder names before the
template ever reaches a customer; missing variables surface as
``render_error`` in the response (still returns 200 — the
dashboard surfaces the error rather than a generic 4xx).

landr-x5o5.5: renders against the per-kind sample context so the
preview is consistent with the variable catalog.

get.description:
Status table over this operator's Holded sync-log rows + bucket summary.

The summary always reflects ALL of this operator's Holded rows (so the
dashboard tab badges are correct); ``bucket``/``limit``/``offset`` page
only the ``rows`` payload.

parameters.description: Filter rows to a single bucket. Summary always covers all.
parameters.name: bucket

schema.description: Filter rows to a single bucket. Summary always covers all.
schema.title: Bucket

post.description:
Run one operator-scoped Holded sync pass synchronously and return counts.

Reuses ``run_holded_sync`` with ``operator_id`` scoping (the worker's
SELECT already narrows to that operator and to pending Holded rows whose
backoff has elapsed). When the operator has no decrypted Holded key we
short-circuit to a ``holded_not_connected`` 200 body — the rows stay
pending and the dashboard renders a connect affordance.

get.description:
ENTER-to-search: return the top ~10 Places Text Search matches.

One billed Text Search call, region-biased to the operator's country, so a
small local hotel surfaces ahead of global same-name results. Each result
is fully hydrated (place_id + the normalized form fields) so picking one
needs no follow-up details call.
Return the current token + URL, auto-creating on first access.

schema.title: Response Get Or Create Operator Ical Token

get.summary: Get Or Create Operator Ical Token

post.description:
Rotate the token (UPSERT) and return the new URL.

Idempotent in the sense that any caller always gets a fresh token;
NOT idempotent in the sense that calling twice publishes two
different URLs and invalidates the first. The dashboard warns the
operator before firing this.

get.description:
Masked list of this operator's credential bundles.

One entry per stored (provider, mode). NEVER returns a decrypted secret.

put.description:
Create or rotate the (provider, mode) credential bundle for this operator.

Secret fields in the body are encrypted server-side via secret_box BEFORE
the row is written. Returns the MASKED row (never the secret).

get.description:
Flat list of the operator's groups including `parent_id`.

The dashboard builds the nested tree client-side from `parent_id`
(adjacency list). Returned ordered by (parent_id, sort_order) so a
naive grouping is already sibling-ordered. Same projection as the
plain list endpoint — `parent_id` is the only field the tree editor
additionally needs and it is already in `_COLUMNS`.

patch.description:
Move a group under a new parent (or to the root with null).

Guards:
- the node itself must exist (404 group_not_found),
- a non-null parent must be an existing non-deleted group of the
  same operator (400 parent_not_found),
- the move must not create a cycle — a node can't become its own
  descendant/ancestor (400 cycle_detected).

get.description:
List Views visible to the caller: every Shared View on this
operator plus every Personal View the caller created. Soft-deleted
rows are excluded. Each row is decorated with the caller's
per-user state (star / hidden).

post.description:
Create a View. creator_user_id is stamped from the caller's
membership (never accepted from the body) — defense in depth on
top of the RLS WITH CHECK.

patch.description:
Bulk-persist per-user sort_order across many Views in one round-trip.

Used by the Gmail-style sidebar (landr-45pb) when the user drags within
the Primary (pinned) bucket — every reorder shuffles N rows and we'd
rather not fire N sequential PUTs.

Semantics:
  * Each item upserts the caller's view_user_state row for that view_id
    with the supplied sort_order. Existing pinned/hidden values are
    preserved; if no row exists yet a fresh one is created with
    pinned=false, hidden=false, sort_order=<supplied>.
  * Each view_id is validated against the operator before write —
    a view that is not on this operator (or is soft-deleted, or is a
    Personal View the caller does not own) is silently skipped.
    (Skipping rather than 4xx keeps the DnD UX forgiving: a stale
    client cache shouldn't poison the rest of the batch.)
  * Returns {"updated": <count>} — the number of rows actually written.

Empty body → 400 (defensive; matches the per-row endpoint).

delete.description: Soft delete: stamp deleted_at. Edit permission enforced in code.

patch.description:
Update name / visibility / config / sort_order. Enforces the
View-editor rule in code (creator OR operator owner) — RLS does
the same, but the explicit 403 gives the dashboard a clean signal.

post.description:
Copy a View's config into a new Personal View owned by the caller.

CONTEXT.md "Duplicate":
  * Original is untouched.
  * Default visibility of the copy is Personal regardless of source.
  * Caller becomes the creator.
  * Name suffixed with " (copy)" (clamped to 200-char column limit).

put.description:
Upsert the caller's view_user_state row (pinned / hidden / sort_order).

Body is partial — omitted fields retain their current value (or
sensible defaults on first write: pinned=false, hidden=false,
sort_order=0). Returns the resulting row.

get.description:
List the operator's service roles ordered by sort_order.

Includes inactive roles (so the manager can re-activate them); only
soft-deleted rows are hidden.

post.description: Create a new service role. `code` must be unique per operator → 409.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a service role (stamp deleted_at).

Guard: deleting the operator's last active role is refused (409). We
look up the target first to (a) 404 cleanly when it's missing /
cross-tenant, and (b) only run the last-role count when the target is
itself active (deleting an already-inactive role can never strand the
widget).

patch.description:
Rename / reorder / (de)activate a service role.

Guard: deactivating the operator's last active role is refused (409)
— the booking widget needs at least one active role.

get.description: List all active tags for an operator. Soft-deleted rows are excluded.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a tag (stamp deleted_at).

The booking_tags / contact_tags junction rows are NOT removed — they
remain referencing the tag id so historical assignments stay
auditable. The UI filters them out via the deleted_at IS NULL join,
so soft-deleted tags simply disappear from chips and pickers.

patch.description: Rename or recolor a tag. Empty patch → 400.

get.description:
List soft-deleted rows of `kind` for the operator.

Ordered newest-deleted-first so the operator sees the items most likely
to be the accidental delete at the top. Capped at 500 rows — the trash
bin is not designed to be a paged historical archive; operators clear
or restore from here and old rows age out via the (not-in-scope here)
cleanup job referenced in the ticket description.

post.description:
Restore one soft-deleted row by flipping deleted_at back to NULL.

Scoped by (id, operator_id, deleted_at IS NOT NULL) so:
  - a row id from another operator → 404 (operator_id mismatch);
  - an already-live row → 404 (the deleted_at IS NOT NULL filter);
  - a non-existent id → 404.

Returns the post-restore row so the dashboard can optimistically remove
it from the trash list without a re-fetch.

get.description:
List all live vouchers for an operator with their used_count.

Soft-deleted rows (deleted_at IS NOT NULL) are excluded.

post.description:
Create a voucher.

`code` is uppercased to satisfy vouchers_code_uppercase_check and the
.eq(UPPER(code)) pricing lookup (landr-hy5t). A duplicate code for the
same operator (among live rows) surfaces as 409. `applies_to_product_id`
/ `campaign_id`, if supplied, must resolve to a live row on this
operator — a dangling or cross-operator id surfaces as 400.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a voucher (stamp deleted_at + flip active false).

Soft delete keeps the row for audit/analytics and historical
booking.voucher_id_applied references intact. The partial unique index
excludes deleted rows, so the operator can mint a fresh voucher with the
same code afterwards.

patch.description:
Edit a voucher. Empty patch → 400. Cross-operator / missing → 404.

`applies_to_product_id` / `campaign_id`, if supplied and non-null, must
resolve to a live row on this operator — a dangling or cross-operator
id surfaces as 400. Explicitly patching either to null always passes
(clearing the scope needs no ownership check).

get.description:
Return a one-day weather forecast hint for the operator's configured location.

Returns ``{enabled: false}`` when weather is disabled for this operator.
Returns ``{enabled: true, error: "..."}`` when weather is enabled but the
provider is unreachable or returns unexpected data.
Returns a full forecast dict when everything succeeds.

parameters.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD to fetch the forecast for.
parameters.name: date

schema.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD to fetch the forecast for.
schema.title: Date

post.description:
Fan @mention notifications out to the listed users, bypassing quiet settings.

The caller MUST have already inserted the comment via the Supabase REST API.
This endpoint handles only the side-effecting notification dispatch.

Auth:
    Any authenticated user (operator or landr-staff).  Returns 401 if the
    JWT is missing/invalid; returns 403 if the caller has no public.users row.

Idempotency:
    Duplicate calls create duplicate bell records.  Callers SHOULD ensure a
    single call per comment (the client fires once after a successful insert).

Returns:
    200 with a dispatch summary (bell_user_ids, skipped, push_sent_to,
    email_enqueued_for).
post.summary: Dispatch @mention notifications for a comment — overrides quiet/silenced per-ticket settings for the mentioned users.

post.description:
PER-OPERATOR endpoint (landr-1nwu.3).

Resolve token -> operator (404 if unknown) BEFORE verification, verify with
THAT operator's env-selected webhook secret (no app-level fallback —
503 if the operator has none), then dispatch the event scoped to this
operator (refuse cross-operator payment_intent ids).
